metadata.description: A DHCP address scope — a subnet's dynamic pool(s), options, and reservations. Grounded in the DHCP protocol concepts (RFC 2131 / RFC 8415). The concrete pool/option/lease model is provider config (any DHCP provider — Kea, dnsmasq, ISC DHCP, a cloud DHCP), projected not dictated; UDLM carries the neutral subnet + the graph edges. UDLM does not prescribe DHCP as the way to serve addresses — an address's origin is a field on Network.IPAddress (static/dhcp/link-layer/random).

context.purpose: Declares a subnet's DHCP configuration — dynamic pools, options, lease time — as the neutral surface any DHCP provider serves.
context.plain_description: One subnet's DHCP setup: the CIDR, the dynamic ranges leased from, common options (gateway, DNS servers, domain name), and lease time. Reservations are not authored here — they are derived: every statically-allocated address record bound to an interface projects into this scope's reservation list, so the fact that an address belongs to a MAC lives in exactly one place. The DHCP server software is a provider; this record is what it renders its config from.
use_when: You need a subnet's dynamic ranges and options declared portably, independent of which DHCP server serves them.
You need static reservations to fall out of address records automatically instead of being maintained twice.
not_for: Allocation-side accounting of a range (who holds which address, is it exhausted) — Network.IPAddressPool; the scope is service-side config. The two overlap on ranges deliberately, and both document it.
One address or reservation — Network.IPAddress with static allocation; it projects into the scope.
works_with: Network.IPAddress — the address records whose static allocations project into reservations.
Network.AddressService — the operated service serving this scope.
Compute.BareMetalHost / Compute.VirtualMachine — the servers the scope is served from.

subnet.description: Scope subnet in CIDR (common-elements: cidr), e.g. 192.0.2.0/24.

pools.description: Dynamic allocation ranges within the subnet (DHCP).

reservations.description: DERIVED — not authored here. A projection of every Network.IPAddress(allocation: static) bound to a Hardware.NetworkInterface (its MAC) contained by a host (its hostname), within this scope. The reservation truth lives on the IPAddress records (ADR-023); a DHCP provider renders its reservations/leases from this set.

relationships.name: dhcp_server_host
relationships.description: Host(s) running the DHCP server(s) serving this scope (e.g. an HA pair).

adopts.role: reference realization — one DHCP provider's subnet/pool/reservation model (MPL-2.0, referenced by name only, not the type's grounding)

aliases.name: DHCP Pool
aliases.note: common term for the dynamic range
